data.title: Greenridge Capital gives Cango Inc (CANG) a buy rating, targeting US$4.00; praises asset-light mining model and AI potential.
data.description: In Dallas, Texas on December 24, 2025, investment firm Greenridge Capital has begun covering Cango Inc. (NYSE: CANG) with a buy rating and a target price of $4.00. The analysis highlights that Cango seems undervalued in the market despite its strong position in the Bitcoin sector.

data.title: Sage Accounting awarded for real-time collaboration and access in UK by Better Business Advice in Jan 2026.
data.description: A recent report by Better Business Advice discusses prominent cloud accounting platforms in the UK, emphasizing the role of these systems in modern finance. The report specifically praises Sage Accounting for its capabilities in providing real-time access to financial data. Released on December 24, 2025, this review sheds light on the growing significance of cloud accounting in the UK market. The report's findings are crucial for businesses seeking efficient financial management solutions.

data.title: Ultimate Pet Nutrition offers savings on Nutra Complete Freeze-Dried Raw Dog Food, along with other top pet products.
data.description: Ultimate Pet Nutrition, a renowned pet wellness brand, has introduced exclusive savings programs that offer exceptional value on Nutra Complete's premium, whole-food nutrition, along with various top pet products. The initiative was announced in Los Angeles on December 24, 2025. This program provides customers with significant discounts and deals to access top-quality pet products.

data.title: Americans participating in the Light the World Giving Machine initiative are donating food, livestock, education, and hygiene gifts for Christmas to aid children, homeless individuals, and families, according to new data.
data.description: Salt Lake City reported data from Giving Machines reveals that Americans are generously donating food, livestock, education, and hygiene items during Christmas to assist children, homeless individuals, and families in need. The donations from individuals are significantly contributing to supporting those in need during the holiday season.

data.title: BP sold 65% of Castrol for $10.1 billion to Stonepeak, unlocking $6 billion to reduce debt and streamline operations.
data.description: BP PLC (NYSE: BP) is strategically divesting its Castrol lubricants business by selling a 65% controlling stake to infrastructure investor Stonepeak. The deal is valued at approximately $10.1 billion, with BP expecting to generate around $6 billion in net proceeds, including an advance linked to dividend income from its remaining stake. This move is part of BP's efforts to streamline its portfolio, enhance its balance sheet, simplify operations, uphold financial discipline, and focus on assets that provide steady cash flow. The company's decision to offload a majority stake in Castrol underscores its commitment to optimizing its business structure for long-term sustainability.

data.title: Jim Cramer criticizes panic selling in high-growth sectors post strong GDP print, calls it 'stupid.' NVIDIA, AI, and crypto stocks affected.
data.description: Financial expert and television personality, Jim Cramer, expressed frustration on Tuesday over the panic caused by robust economic data impacting high-growth sectors like artificial intelligence and cryptocurrency. Cramer criticized the knee-jerk reaction as unwarranted, labeling it a continuous "big freakout" that affects companies such as NVIDIA Corp. (NASDAQ: NVDA) and those in the cryptocurrency, quantum computing, and data center spaces. The "Mad Money" host described the situation as "stupid," emphasizing how a strong economic number leads to declines in stocks related to NVIDIA, cryptocurrency, quantum technology, rockets, and data centers. Cramer's comments reflect his disbelief in the ongoing negative market response to positive economic data.
